symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt-onset, regular, very rapid palpitations (~180 bpm) at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt-onset, regular, very fast palpitations are characteristic of paroxysmal re-entrant SVT.
The abrupt onset and disproportionate rate at rest argue against sinus tachycardia.
A sustained, non-self-terminating episode needs prompt medical termination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness/presyncope and mild exertional dyspnoea during tachycardia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presyncope reflects reduced cardiac output at very high rates but, without hypotension/syncope, does not indicate instability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No severe chest pain and no syncope (haemodynamically stable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chest pain, syncope and hypotension indicates a currently stable SVT suitable for vagal manoeuvre and urgent (not immediately emergent) termination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent prior self-terminating episodes; heavy caffeine intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recurrent paroxysmal pattern and caffeine as a trigger fit re-entrant SVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Short PR interval (<120 ms), narrow QRS without delta wave, and the tachycardia morphology on 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: Regular narrow-complex tachycardia; post-conversion short PR (<120 ms) with a normal narrow QRS and no delta wave (LGL pattern), distinguishing it from WPW
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The arrhythmia mechanism is not captured and the diagnosis (and need for electrophysiology) cannot be confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ECG captures the tachycardia and the short-PR/narrow-QRS pattern defining the mechanism.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: An abrupt, regular, very fast tachycardia sustained >30 minutes with presyncope but no instability indicates stable SVT - coach a vagal manoeuvre and arrange urgent ECG/adenosine, escalating if unstable.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Captures the rhythm mechanism; must be obtained urgently while the heart is racing.
Required for safe pharmacological termination (adenosine) and to detect any instability.
For definitive diagnosis and potential curative ablation of recurrent SVT, arranged after the acute episode.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance to wait it out at home without ECG capture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A sustained SVT should have the rhythm captured and terminated; waiting misses diagnosis and risks decompensation.
